title: codonoboea urticoides
intro: Codonoboea urticoides, also known as the 'stinging cat's paw', is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique stinging hairs. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. These properties have been associated with various health benefits, including pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and minor injuries.
